item.title: Re: Tag missing?
item.content:encoded: Tag added, thank you for pointing it out.

item.title: Tag missing?
item.content:encoded: I believe this article was featured but it doesn't have the tag "featured". Is this a mistake or is it deliberate?

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Both images are property of Porter Sculpture Park. As neither are stated to be released under CC 3.0, they are assumed not compliant and have since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one is pretty cool. I believe they worship -1 like a god.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tag has been added!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Superficial and structural damage to SCP-1175-1 will repair itself through unknown means.

Permission to add the "self-repairing" tag?

item.title: Not Understanding
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I don't get this one. It's interesting, it's written all right, but the thing just seems to be a bunch of random stuff cobbled together (granted, I could say that about every SCP, but more so than usual). It reminds of me if that Bovine Heart, which was just weird. Am I missing a backstory?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh… that makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are We Cool Yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a bit confused…. who's "you know who"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a lot of art SCPs now. It is hard to write one now without sounding like you are copying some other SCP. It is becoming a cliche. That is why it is harder to write good SCPs because people like this guy take all of the good ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great. I like the combination of elements. It's dangerous whilst not being outwardly hostile, the motivations of -1 and the -2s are unclear, but their behaviours are still predictable. It's almost sweet how the objects will be friendly to someone if they're introduced, but then creepy how, even though the -2s don't deliberately seek to hunt/kill to feed -1, they will still feed the bodies of unwelcome intruders to it.

A well-deserved +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Came for the feature, stayed for the 'Taurs. Well upvoted. This was cool from the start (see what I did there?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stingy Foundation is always a win.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I'm glad this is featured so more people can pay attention to it, I love this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since the -2's are made of various metals, what about using a strong electromagnet to restrain them and then doing whatever would normally activate them. A logical rebuttal could be something like "They aren't made of ferromagnetic metals" or "Their strength far exceeds the forces of any available electromagnets" but I would like to hear your thoughts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that the Foundation is charging admission to pay for the guards. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepy! The pics definitely add a lot to it as well, and I like that they have a "leader" so to speak. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cow Power! I dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot happening with this one, but it has a twisted internal logic that we're given just a surface glimpse of. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. This right here.

It feels like a Twilight Zone piece, with the park owner politely introducing an agent to these ten foot tall skeleton guards. I love this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My main issue with most of my ideas is that I tend to get a bit… Rambly. I end up posting exceeding detail about certain things, and not posting enough about the actual stuff. I've found that going bare-bones and patching slowly over time seems to make it work out best, at least when posting on this site.

I thought I had already addressed KC's issues (If a major change in the park happens the -2's wake up immediately, and alternate means to move the bull head were either failures or directly stopped by the -2's) but if there's more, let me know. And as far as the artist giving permission, that was why I posted that they follow his orders "to some extent." If he says this guy's okay to mess around in the park after nightfall and to touch the statue, they'll let him; if he says "Hey, I've got a new piece of art that I want to put up, and it's going over here", there's about a 50/50 shot that they'll let it stand; if he says "So um, yeah, we're just gonna move this whole thing inside a big building in the middle of nowhere," no, they're not going to follow that one.

For once, I actually don't have a big meta backstory in mind; it's just a batch of semi-surrealist metalworking art, 7 pieces of which spontaneously gained some sort of autonomous intelligence, that seems to like everything around them staying more-or-less exactly the way it is. They're not OPENLY hostile to humans, but they're untrusting, and HEY YOU KIDS DON'T TOUCH THE BULL OH god dammit Steve go cut their heads off! (or, as we hear it, RREEEEEEEEHHHHHHH)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, though I would like to see it cleaned up a bit for internal logic. (the things Khym Chanur said especially. Also what happens if the artist gave permission for it to be relocated to SCP holdings? They don't need an answer but feel like holes in the narrative to me.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, since the "E" is a temporary designation for people assigned to the retrieval and initial study of an object before it becomes an official SCP, and this is already an official SCP, I don't really think it applies here.. ;) You'll probably only see E-class in tales and recovery logs.

"D" isn't like a grade, with C B and A over it. D stands for… Well. It's not official, but, "disposable".

Edit: Somehow forgot to mention: If Mr. P was to be given an actual Foundation job and ranking, he'd probably be level 1 (most junior research/maintenance, only allowed to be attached to a single project at a time).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally believe this should be added to the actual article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If there's anything specific you'd care to point out, feel free and I'll work on it. Or just, go ahead and do it yourself.

I expanded a bit more on the pillars, but not much; they're SUPPOSED to be a bit "Now… Not that ANY of it makes much sense, but why is it making extensions into the ground? Stabilizers? Roots? Communication? Mineral extraction?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good point about the excavation; I added that to the "-2 basically chest-bumped them until they either knocked it off, or things got hostile" list. No that's not actually what happened, but I'm tired and silly at the moment.

I also added the fact that the -2's will go on alert when something gets messed up, even if previously inactive, which I could have sworn I put in the first time o_O Well, as I've apparently come to always say, the streak continues!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does it become active if anyone attempts to remove the -1 or -2 during the day on non-Tuesdays? Because if not, then the -2s could simply be removed during the day.

And if the bull statue can't be removed by a crane, the obvious next step (to me, at least) would be to excavate the dirt around the statue until there's nothing holding it up, let if fall over, and roll it up onto a flat-bed truck. I mean, of course the attempt would fail in some way, but I can't see the foundation not giving it a try.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well he can't pass on the ability to "introduce" people so he is part of the phenomenon and not just an agent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He… he made… it's a giant minotaur man. With little peoples that like… protect it or some… thing?

That just ain't cool.

I mean props to coming to life but… that's just… whatever the opposite of cool is.

And you really want to take credit for it?

Man.

Okay. You do what you gotta do. I'm gonna make an artistic toilet that eats people.

Are we cool yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Minotaur was inside you all along.

Are we cool yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for encouragement. I think this could use some cleaning up, mostly for phrasings and sentence structure. I'd also like a little more about those pillars; they're mentioned once and then never expounded on.

I agree with the 'internal logic' comment above, that's what makes this work. Also, the word 'art' appears in this and AWCY does not, and I didn't think you were allowed to do that anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. It was an interesting idea actually. But my one, tiny and nitpicky issue is the creator's designation as 1175-3, rather than an E-Class. Something about the lack of E-Class agents agitates me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good SCP, though it reminds me a bit of night at the museum, but it's different enough from that that I give a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had played around with different sizes, it started outright obstructing and screwing up the format of the rest of it. I may work a bit of Photoshoppery when I get home to crop it down some and enhance the detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. The idea of the park owner being part of the SCP is fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God, that second image is far too small.

item.title: You of all should know, Sorts
item.content:encoded: If it doesn't wave its dick, it ain't art.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We should really make more art-based SCPs that aren't AWCY because they really don't need to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was considering doing that, then I realized "Wait a minute…. What if…" and having him be their unwitting liaison just popped into my head XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incident report 1175-03: On [blankity blank], four individuals entered the park; two created a distraction while two proceeded to rush SCP-1175-1 and deface it with spray paint. "Are We" was all that was accomplished before SCP-1175-2 activated, attacked and killed those involved, and fed them to -1. -1 proceeded to spit out the remains.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yessss an art-based SCP that is weird and follows its own internal logic without being necessarily deadly nor does it smirk and wink at you while waving its dick around.

Thank you ChazzK. Thank you.

(in b4 someone says AWCY would take credit for it anyway because lolart)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. Also, keeping the park director as a liaison, instead of the usual "amnesiac the hell out of everyone, and worry about the consequences later." The idea of an art display in a park becoming sentient, and using vandals for food… :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bam, here we go. A long time idea finally inspired by something that's… related to my family. If folks recognize where it's actually from then you'll get a guess what I mean.

And before folks jump to conclusions: yes, it's an artistic piece. No, it does not, and shall not ever, have any relations to you-know-who.
